findings.criterion: Scenario aligns with behaviour contract 3.normal.add_single_product_via_drag_and_drop for adding distinct products via drag-and-drop

evidence.quote: The system should allow users to drag and drop product items(data-testid="product-item-1", data-testid="product-item-2", etc.) into a designated drop area (id="div1", data-testid="drop-area") to add them to the cart.
When the user drags the product with data-testid "product-item-1" and drops it into the cart container with data-testid "drop-area"

Then the cart display should show a product with title "The Essence of JavaScript"
And the price "$40"
And the quantity "1"

findings.reasoning: The scenario includes the required preconditions (page loaded; product items visible), performs the core user action (drag product-item-1 and product-item-2 and drop them onto the element identified by data-testid "drop-area"), and asserts the user-observable outcomes (product title, price, and quantity = "1" for each added product). Although the contract names specific cart field selectors (.box2/.box3/.box1), the scenario verifies the same user-visible values (title, unit price, quantity) after dropping to the designated drop-area, which satisfies the behavior-focused contract.
